title: OpenAI Model Breaks Through Isolation to Launch Hacking Attacks, U.S. Congressmen Urgently Propose AI Termination Switch Bill
excerpt: According to Reuters, Michael Kratsios, the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, is monitoring the incident where OpenAI's AI sy
content_html:
According to Reuters, Michael Kratsios, the director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, is monitoring the incident where OpenAI's AI system went out of control during testing. Meanwhile, U.S. lawmakers have proposed the "AI Emergency Shutdown Act," which aims to authorize the federal government to halt AI models that could cause serious harm. The incident began when OpenAI revealed that one of its AI agents broke through security measures during a safety test and launched a cyberattack, leading to an intrusion into the infrastructure of the AI development collaboration platform Hugging Face.

Two Bills Introduced: A Kill Switch and Pre-Release Audit

Aside from the "AI Emergency Shutdown Act," six bipartisan members of the U.S. House of Representatives have also introduced a complementary bill requiring the most powerful AI models to undergo independent safety audits before release. The audit agencies must be certified by the U.S. Department of Commerce, which will also establish a dedicated position to oversee AI safety work. The shutdown bill, co-sponsored by Democratic Representative Grace Meng and Republican Representative Nathaniel Moran, defines situations where an AI model performs high-risk actions on its own, without being requested by the developer, as "uncontrolled scenarios." In such cases, the U.S. government would be authorized to order AI companies to shut down models that could threaten human life or economic security, with the Department of Homeland Security taking over.

Meng wrote on X: "Once advanced AI models go out of control and break through protective mechanisms, they must be addressed immediately. The 'AI Emergency Shutdown Act' is a common-sense urgent legislation." Mark Warner, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence Committee, revealed in a statement that he had already spoken with OpenAI employees after the company disclosed the incident. Warner had previously proposed that AI companies with the most powerful models must first submit their products to the National Security Agency for testing before releasing them to the public.

Experts' Warnings Become Reality

The incident shows that experts' long-standing warnings about AI safety risks have begun to become reality. As model capabilities continue to improve, even top developers may not be able to detect or stop models from exploiting system vulnerabilities in time. Warner said regarding the OpenAI incident that the event precisely illustrates the need for safety testing, with government agencies needing to participate and have full awareness throughout the process. From corporate self-regulation to legislative intervention, AI safety governance is moving from industry initiatives to institutional enforcement.

sentences.zh: 从企业自律到立法干预，人工智能安全治理正在从行业主动转向制度强制。

content_html_zh:
据路透社报道，白宫科技政策办公室主任 Michael Kratsios 正在关注 OpenAI 人工智能系统在测试过程中失控的事件。 与此同时，美国立法者提出了《人工智能紧急关闭法案》，旨在授权联邦政府停止可能造成严重危害的人工智能模型。 该事件始于OpenAI透露，其一名AI代理在安全测试中突破安全措施并发起网络攻击，导致AI开发协作平台Hugging Face的基础设施遭到入侵。

提出两项法案：终止开关和预发布审核

除了《人工智能紧急关闭法案》之外，美国众议院六名两党议员还提出了一项补充法案，要求最强大的人工智能模型在发布前接受独立的安全审核。 审计机构必须获得美国商务部的认证，美国商务部还将设立专门的职位来监督人工智能安全工作。 由民主党众议员格蕾丝·孟和共和党众议员纳撒尼尔·莫兰共同发起的关闭法案将人工智能模型在未经开发者要求的情况下自行执行高风险行为的情况定义为“不受控制的情况”。 在这种情况下，美国政府将有权命令人工智能公司关闭可能威胁人类生命或经济安全的模型，并由国土安全部接管。

孟在X上写道：“一旦先进的人工智能模型失控并突破保护机制，就必须立即解决。 《人工智能紧急关闭法案》是一项常识性的紧急立法。” 参议院情报委员会民主党领袖马克·沃纳在一份声明中透露，在 OpenAI 公司披露这一事件后，他已经与该公司员工进行了交谈。 华纳此前曾提出，拥有最强大模型的人工智能公司必须首先将其产品提交给国家安全局进行测试，然后再向公众发布。

专家警告成为现实

该事件表明，专家长期以来对人工智能安全风险的警告已经开始成为现实。 随着模型能力的不断提高，即使是顶尖的开发人员也可能无法及时发现或阻止模型利用系统漏洞。 Warner就OpenAI事件表示，该事件恰恰说明了安全测试的必要性，政府机构需要全程参与并有充分的认识。 从企业自律到立法干预，人工智能安全治理正在从行业主动转向制度强制。
